mpm_family.name: Patrick
mpm_family.preferences: No mushrooms, no olives, only seafood he likes is fish and chips (or similar), peanut butter upsets his stomach (but can handle small amounts), too much tomato sauce in a row upsets his stomach, dislikes cooked peas and cooked carrots

mpm_family.name: Mei
mpm_family.preferences: Limit spice, but still likes Mexican food or a small kick

meals.name: Mom bringing christmas Ham and Dressing, etc
Classic Shepherd's Pie with Beef and Potato
meals.description: A comforting Shepherd's Pie featuring a hearty beef filling topped with creamy mashed potatoes. A classic dish, perfect for a family dinner.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large skillet, brown ground beef over medium-high heat. Drain off any excess grease.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.

Dissolve beef stock concentrate in 1 cup of water and add to the skillet. Bring to a simmer, then stir in Worcestershire sauce, frozen peas, and shredded carrots. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.
While the beef filling simmers, peel and chop the potatoes. Boil them in salted water until tender, about 15-20 minutes. Drain well.
Mash the potatoes with butter and milk until smooth and creamy. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the beef filling into a baking dish.
Spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the beef filling.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the mashed potatoes.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Let cool slightly before serving.
meals.dietary_adaptations: For a lower-carb option, replace the mashed potatoes with mashed cauliflower. To reduce dairy, use dairy-free milk and butter alternatives.
meals.storage_instructions: Store leftover Shepherd's Pie in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
meals.reheating_tips: Reheat in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until heated through. You can also microwave individual portions for 2-3 minutes.

meals.name: Hoisin Glazed Ground Beef and Broccoli Stir-fry with Rice
meals.description: A quick and flavorful stir-fry using ground beef and frozen broccoli, glazed with a homemade hoisin sauce. Served over rice.

ingredients.item: Birds eye frozen broccoli stir fry mix

steps: Cook rice according to package directions.
In a bowl, whisk together hoisin sauce, soy sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, ginger (if available), and cornstarch. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Brown the ground beef, breaking it up with a spoon.
Add the frozen broccoli stir fry mix to the skillet. Cook until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Pour the hoisin sauce mixture over the beef and broccoli. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens.
Stir in sesame oil and red pepper flakes (if using).
Serve over cooked rice.
meals.dietary_adaptations: To reduce sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce and limit the amount of hoisin sauce. Patrick can eat this as peanut butter is not the main component, but serve a smaller portion size to be cautious.
meals.storage_instructions: Store leftover stir-fry and rice separately in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
meals.reheating_tips: Reheat stir-fry in a skillet or microwave. Reheat rice in the microwave with a tablespoon of water to prevent it from drying out.

meals.name: Zesty Southwest Chicken & Bean Tacos
meals.description: A quick, hearty taco night using fresh chicken breast, fiber-packed beans, and healthy fats from avocado.
steps: Dice chicken breast into small bite-sized pieces.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add chicken, chili powder, and cumin. Sauté until browned.
Stir in the drained beans and the can of Rotel (with juices). Simmer for 10 minutes until liquid reduces.
While chicken simmers, shred the romaine lettuce and slice the avocados.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet.
Assemble tacos with chicken mixture, lettuce, and avocado.
meals.family_adaptation: Keep the cheese in a separate bowl. Mei should skip the cheese to remain dairy-free. All other components are safe.
meals.storage_instructions: Store chicken and bean mixture separately from the tortillas and fresh toppings.
meals.reheating_tips: Warm the chicken mixture on the stove. Use fresh tortillas and avocado for the leftovers.

meals.name: Asian Peanut Noodle Stir-Fry
meals.description: A quick-fix dinner using pantry staples and whole grain pasta for a healthy twist on satay noodles.
steps: Boil the spaghetti according to package instructions.
While pasta cooks, dice chicken and sauté in a large pan with a splash of vegetable oil until cooked through.
Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, sesame oil, honey, and lemon juice (add a splash of hot pasta water to thin it into a sauce).
Add frozen peas and shredded carrots to the chicken pan and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Drain pasta and toss into the pan with the chicken, veggies, and peanut sauce until well coated.
meals.family_adaptation: This dish is dairy-free as long as peanut butter and oil are used for the sauce instead of butter.
meals.storage_instructions: Store in a single container. The noodles will absorb the sauce, so it may be thicker the next day.
meals.reheating_tips: Add a tablespoon of water or broth before reheating in the microwave to loosen the peanut sauce.

meals.name: Slow Cooker White Chicken & Bean Chili
meals.description: A protein-packed, comforting chili using chicken breast and northern beans. It's hearty and easy to scale for leftovers.
steps: Place chicken breasts in the bottom of the slow cooker.
Finely dice the onion and bell peppers (if purchased) and add to the pot.
Add drained beans, salsa, garlic, chicken stock concentrate, cumin, chili powder, and water.
Cook on low for 6 hours or high for 3 hours until chicken is tender.
Remove chicken, shred with two forks, and return to the pot.
Stir well and season with salt and pepper to taste before serving.
meals.family_adaptation: This recipe is naturally dairy-free for Mei. Elisa and Patrick can top their servings with shredded Mexican cheese and a dollop of Litehouse Ranch or sour cream for added richness.
meals.storage_instructions: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.
meals.reheating_tips: Reheat in a microwave-safe bowl for 2-3 minutes, stirring halfway through. If too thick, add a splash of water or chicken broth.

mpm_favorites.name: Creamy Chicken and Peas Pasta Bake
mpm_favorites.description: A comforting pasta bake featuring chicken, peas, and a creamy sauce made with Campbell's Cream of Chicken Soup.
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Cook macaroni according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
While pasta is cooking, melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in flour and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in milk until smooth. Stir in Campbell's Cream of Chicken Soup, salt, and pepper. Simmer until thickened.
In a large bowl, combine cooked macaroni, grilled chicken, sweet peas, and creamy sauce. Mix well.
Pour mixture into a greased 9x13 inch baking dish.
Top with shredded cheese and breadcrumbs (if using).
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and bubbly.
mpm_favorites.dietary_adaptations: To reduce fat, use fat-free milk and reduced-fat cheese. Elisa can eat this in smaller portions, and with non-dairy milk alternative.
mpm_favorites.storage_instructions: Store leftover pasta bake in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_favorites.reheating_tips: Reheat in the oven or microwave until heated through.

mpm_favorites.name: Hoisin Glazed Ground Beef and Broccoli Stir-fry with Rice
mpm_favorites.description: A quick and flavorful stir-fry using ground beef and frozen broccoli, glazed with a homemade hoisin sauce. Served over rice.

ingredients.item: Birds eye frozen broccoli stir fry mix

steps: Cook rice according to package directions.
In a bowl, whisk together hoisin sauce, soy sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, ginger (if available), and cornstarch. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Brown the ground beef, breaking it up with a spoon.
Add the frozen broccoli stir fry mix to the skillet. Cook until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Pour the hoisin sauce mixture over the beef and broccoli. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens.
Stir in sesame oil and red pepper flakes (if using).
Serve over cooked rice.
mpm_favorites.dietary_adaptations: To reduce sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce and limit the amount of hoisin sauce. Patrick can eat this as peanut butter is not the main component, but serve a smaller portion size to be cautious.
mpm_favorites.storage_instructions: Store leftover stir-fry and rice separately in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_favorites.reheating_tips: Reheat stir-fry in a skillet or microwave. Reheat rice in the microwave with a tablespoon of water to prevent it from drying out.

mpm_favorites.name: Chicken, Pea, and Sweet Potato Curry
mpm_favorites.description: A flavorful and nutritious curry using frozen chicken, canned peas, and sweet potatoes. Uses rice as a base.
steps: Cook rice according to package directions. Set aside.
Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces. Peel and dice sweet potatoes and yellow onion.
Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add chicken and cook until browned on all sides.
Add diced yellow onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and ginger paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and curry powder and cook for 1 minute.
Add diced sweet potatoes and chicken stock concentrate. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until sweet potatoes are tender and chicken is cooked through.
Stir in frozen peas and cook for 5 minutes, or until heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve over rice.
mpm_favorites.storage_instructions: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_favorites.reheating_tips: Reheat in a saucepan over medium heat, or microwave in 1-minute intervals until heated through. Add a splash of water or chicken broth if needed to prevent sticking.

mpm_favorites.name: Hearty Beef and Bean Chili with Cornbread
mpm_favorites.description: A filling chili featuring ground beef, kidney beans, and a touch of sweetness from Del Monte Petite Diced Tomatoes. Served with cornbread for a satisfying meal.
steps: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned.
Add diced onions and minced garlic to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in chili powder and cumin and cook for 1 minute more.
Add diced tomatoes and kidney beans to the pot. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat and simmer for at least 30 minutes, or up to 1 hour, stirring occasionally. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
While chili is simmering, prepare the cornbread according to the package directions. (Usually involves mixing the corn muffin mix with egg and milk).
Bake cornbread until golden brown, let cool slightly.
Serve chili hot with a slice of warm cornbread.
mpm_favorites.dietary_adaptations: To reduce fat, use lean ground beef. Patrick should avoid too much tomato sauce, so it's important to not serve this multiple times in a row.
mpm_favorites.storage_instructions: Store chili in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days. Store cornbread separately at room temperature for up to 2 days.
mpm_favorites.reheating_tips: Reheat chili on the stovetop or in the microwave. Reheat cornbread in the oven or microwave.

mpm_favorites.name: Hearty Beef and Lentil 'Shepherd' Stew
mpm_favorites.description: A thick, protein-rich stew combining ground beef and lentils, served with diced potatoes for a complete meal.
steps: In a large pot, brown the ground beef with diced onions and garlic over medium heat; drain excess fat.
Add diced potatoes (small cubes), shredded carrots, rinsed lentils, crushed tomatoes, and beef concentrate.
Add 6 cups of water and Worcestershire sauce.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes until lentils and potatoes are tender.
Adjust consistency with more water if needed, and season with salt and black pepper.
mpm_favorites.family_adaptation: This recipe is dairy-free. Elisa and Patrick can sprinkle Kroger Parmesan Cheese on top when serving.
mpm_favorites.storage_instructions: Keep in a sealed container in the fridge for 3-4 days. This stew tastes even better the next day.
mpm_favorites.reheating_tips: Warm on the stovetop over medium heat, adding a splash of water as the lentils will absorb liquid while sitting.

mpm_favorites.name: Slow Cooker White Chicken & Bean Chili
mpm_favorites.description: A protein-packed, comforting chili using chicken breast and northern beans. It's hearty and easy to scale for leftovers.
steps: Place chicken breasts in the bottom of the slow cooker.
Finely dice the onion and bell peppers (if purchased) and add to the pot.
Add drained beans, salsa, garlic, chicken stock concentrate, cumin, chili powder, and water.
Cook on low for 6 hours or high for 3 hours until chicken is tender.
Remove chicken, shred with two forks, and return to the pot.
Stir well and season with salt and pepper to taste before serving.
mpm_favorites.family_adaptation: This recipe is naturally dairy-free for Mei. Elisa and Patrick can top their servings with shredded Mexican cheese and a dollop of Litehouse Ranch or sour cream for added richness.
mpm_favorites.storage_instructions: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.
mpm_favorites.reheating_tips: Reheat in a microwave-safe bowl for 2-3 minutes, stirring halfway through. If too thick, add a splash of water or chicken broth.

mpm_leftovers.name: Patrick's Salad
mpm_leftovers.tip: Refrigerate in airtight container. Good for 3-4 days.
mpm_leftovers.reheat: Microwave 2-3 minutes or reheat on stovetop.

mpm_recipes_cache.name: Classic Shepherd's Pie with Beef and Potato
mpm_recipes_cache.description: A comforting Shepherd's Pie featuring a hearty beef filling topped with creamy mashed potatoes. A classic dish, perfect for a family dinner.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large skillet, brown ground beef over medium-high heat. Drain off any excess grease.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.

Dissolve beef stock concentrate in 1 cup of water and add to the skillet. Bring to a simmer, then stir in Worcestershire sauce, frozen peas, and shredded carrots. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.
While the beef filling simmers, peel and chop the potatoes. Boil them in salted water until tender, about 15-20 minutes. Drain well.
Mash the potatoes with butter and milk until smooth and creamy. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the beef filling into a baking dish.
Spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the beef filling.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the mashed potatoes.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Let cool slightly before serving.
mpm_recipes_cache.dietary_adaptations: For a lower-carb option, replace the mashed potatoes with mashed cauliflower. To reduce dairy, use dairy-free milk and butter alternatives.
mpm_recipes_cache.storage_instructions: Store leftover Shepherd's Pie in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_recipes_cache.reheating_tips: Reheat in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until heated through. You can also microwave individual portions for 2-3 minutes.

mpm_recipes_cache.name: Shepherd's Pie with Beef and Sweet Potato Topping
mpm_recipes_cache.description: A twist on the classic Shepherd's Pie, featuring a hearty beef filling topped with mashed sweet potatoes for a slightly sweeter and more nutritious twist.
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large skillet, brown ground beef over medium-high heat. Drain off any excess grease.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.

Dissolve beef stock concentrate in 1 cup of water and add to the skillet. Bring to a simmer, then stir in Worcestershire sauce, frozen peas, and shredded carrots. Season with cumin, salt and pepper to taste. Simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.
While the beef filling simmers, peel and chop the sweet potatoes. Boil them in salted water until tender, about 15-20 minutes. Drain well.
Mash the sweet potatoes with butter and milk until smooth and creamy. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the beef filling into a baking dish.
Spread the mashed sweet potatoes evenly over the beef filling.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the mashed sweet potatoes.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Let cool slightly before serving.
mpm_recipes_cache.dietary_adaptations: For a vegan option, replace the ground beef with lentils or plant-based crumbles and use dairy-free butter and milk alternatives. Omit the cheese or use a vegan cheese alternative.
mpm_recipes_cache.storage_instructions: Store leftover Shepherd's Pie in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_recipes_cache.reheating_tips: Reheat in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until heated through. You can also microwave individual portions for 2-3 minutes.

mpm_recipes_cache.name: Shepherd's Pie with Beef and Broccoli Potato Mash
mpm_recipes_cache.description: A Shepherd's Pie variation adding broccoli to the potato mash for extra nutrients and texture, while maintaining the hearty beef base.

ingredients.item: Birds Eye Frozen Broccoli Stir Fry Mix

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large skillet, brown ground beef over medium-high heat. Drain off any excess grease.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.

Dissolve beef stock concentrate in 1 cup of water and add to the skillet. Bring to a simmer, then stir in Worcestershire sauce, frozen peas, and shredded carrots. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.

While the beef filling simmers, peel and chop the potatoes. Boil them in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes. Steam broccoli stir fry mix until mostly cooked, about 5 minutes. Drain both well.
Mash the potatoes and broccoli together with butter and milk until smooth and creamy. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the beef filling into a baking dish.
Spread the mashed potato and broccoli mixture evenly over the beef filling.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the potato and broccoli mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Let cool slightly before serving.
mpm_recipes_cache.dietary_adaptations: For a vegan option, substitute the ground beef with lentils or plant-based crumbles. Use dairy-free butter, milk, and cheese alternatives. Make sure the Worcestershire sauce is vegan-friendly (many contain anchovies).
mpm_recipes_cache.storage_instructions: Store leftover Shepherd's Pie in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_recipes_cache.reheating_tips: Reheat in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 15-20 minutes, or microwave individual portions for 2-3 minutes.

mpm_history.name: Hoisin Glazed Ground Beef and Broccoli Stir-fry with Rice
mpm_history.tip: Store leftover stir-fry and rice separately in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_history.reheat: Reheat stir-fry in a skillet or microwave. Reheat rice in the microwave with a tablespoon of water to prevent it from drying out.

mpm_history.name: Hoisin Glazed Ground Beef and Broccoli Stir-fry with Rice
mpm_history.description: A quick and flavorful stir-fry using ground beef and frozen broccoli, glazed with a homemade hoisin sauce. Served over rice.

ingredients.item: Birds eye frozen broccoli stir fry mix

steps: Cook rice according to package directions.
In a bowl, whisk together hoisin sauce, soy sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, ginger (if available), and cornstarch. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Brown the ground beef, breaking it up with a spoon.
Add the frozen broccoli stir fry mix to the skillet. Cook until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Pour the hoisin sauce mixture over the beef and broccoli. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens.
Stir in sesame oil and red pepper flakes (if using).
Serve over cooked rice.
mpm_history.dietary_adaptations: To reduce sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce and limit the amount of hoisin sauce. Patrick can eat this as peanut butter is not the main component, but serve a smaller portion size to be cautious.
mpm_history.storage_instructions: Store leftover stir-fry and rice separately in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
mpm_history.reheating_tips: Reheat stir-fry in a skillet or microwave. Reheat rice in the microwave with a tablespoon of water to prevent it from drying out.
